intro: We bridge rigorous computational biophysics with state-of-the-art biological foundation models to understand and engineer biological systems at atomic resolution. Our work spans fundamental questions in structural biology to translational applications in precision medicine, immunotherapy, and drug discovery - creating the science that connects academic excellence with real-world impact.

directions.title: Structural Bioinformatics & Foundation Models
directions.question: How can we decode the language of protein structure and function at scale?
directions.description: We develop and apply large-scale foundation models trained on protein sequences, structures, and evolutionary data to understand molecular function, predict structural dynamics, and generate novel biomolecules. By integrating deep learning with physics-based reasoning, we push the boundaries of what computational approaches can reveal about life at the atomic scale.

directions.title: Computational Biophysics & Molecular Dynamics
directions.question: How do molecular motions govern biological function and drug action?
directions.description: We use molecular dynamics simulations, enhanced sampling methods, and free energy calculations to characterize protein conformational landscapes, allosteric mechanisms, and ligand binding thermodynamics.

directions.title: Precision Medicine & Immunotherapy
directions.question: How can we tailor therapies to individual molecular and genomic profiles?
directions.description: We integrate multi-omics data with structural and molecular insights to identify patient-specific vulnerabilities, predict treatment response, and accelerate the design of personalized vaccines and immunotherapies - including neoantigen identification and mRNA vaccine optimization.

directions.title: Dynamics-Based Drug Discovery
directions.question: How can we exploit protein dynamics and targeted protein degradation to find next-generation therapeutics?
directions.description: We apply dynamics-driven computational strategies - including cryptic pocket detection, induced-fit docking, and PROTAC/molecular glue design - to identify and optimize small molecules that modulate challenging targets, including intrinsically disordered proteins and protein–protein interfaces.
